metadata.summary: Six-step framework to assess market viability: define niche, estimate TAM/SAM/SOM with citations, map competitors, score accessibility, evaluate monetization, rate personal fit.

content.title: Niche Evaluation
content.summary: Six-step framework to assess whether a market segment is viable: define the niche with a one-sentence formula, estimate TAM/SAM/SOM with cited sources, map competitors on a quality-vs-players matrix, score audience accessibility, evaluate monetization potential, and rate personal fit. Produces a weighted score (0-5) with a decision band; final go/no-go is always human.

why.p: Solopreneurs pick niches too broad (competing with giants) or too small (no revenue) based on gut feeling. The two-phase evidence-before-score discipline — gather citations first, score only from evidence — prevents the #1 agent failure: hallucinated TAM/SAM numbers. Personal fit is the only criterion that cannot be delegated to an agent; any niche with founder fit below 3 fails regardless of score.

list.item: After idea-generation produces 5-10 candidate niches; need to score and short-list.
Pivoting: benchmarking new candidate against current niche performance.
Product-line expansion: evaluating adjacent niche for second product.
Investor/board ask requiring defensible market-sizing with cited sources.
Already shipping with paying customers — churn data beats any scoring model.
Pure curiosity research — the framework is decision-forcing, not exploratory.
Hyper-local services (plumber, dentist) — catchment-area metrics apply, not TAM/SAM/SOM.

rule.statement: Use exactly this formula for niche definition before any evaluation: "[Product Type] for [Specific Audience] who [Specific Problem]". If you cannot write this in one sentence, the niche is not ready to evaluate.
rule.rationale: Vague niche definitions produce vague competitor lists and vague TAM estimates. The formula forces specificity that makes evaluation deterministic rather than opinion-driven.

example.title: Too broad — evaluation will be meaningless

example.code: "Task tracking for freelance designers who struggle with client deadline management"

section.name: market-sizing

rule.statement: Require a source URL for every TAM/SAM/SOM number. Return "unknown" for any number without a citation. Fabricated market sizes are the #1 failure mode in niche evaluation agents.
rule.rationale: LLMs anchor on round-number priors ($1B, $100M, $10M) drawn from startup deck patterns in training data. These are confabulation. "Unknown" is always preferable to a confident wrong number.

section.p: Market sizing targets: TAM (total addressable market) greater than $1B, SAM (serviceable addressable market) greater than $100M, SOM (serviceable obtainable market) greater than $1M. SOM is a multi-year horizon, not Year 1 revenue.

section.name: validation-signals
section.p: Three quick checks before committing to full evaluation:

list.item: Google Trends: is interest growing (up-arrow = good), stable, or declining? Direction matters more than absolute search volume for early-stage evaluation.
Keyword search volume: are people searching for the problem or solution? Zero search volume may mean no demand OR a problem people do not know how to name.
Reddit/community size: is there a community discussing this problem? Measure by 30-day post velocity, not member count — member count is vanity.

section.name: competition-matrix
section.p: Map competitors on two axes: Quality (low/high) x Number of Players (low/high). Best position: high quality, few competitors (Opportunity Zone). Avoid: low quality, many players (Race to Bottom).

rule.statement: Force a "long-tail competitor scan" as a separate pass after the main competitor list. Ask: "what would a $1k MRR solo developer build in this niche?" to surface Indie Hackers, Etsy, and Discord competitors that LLMs miss.
rule.rationale: Agent competitor lists contain the 5 well-known venture-backed names. The actual competition for a solopreneur is the long tail of small operators with overlapping but not identical positioning. Omitting them produces false "blue ocean" conclusions.

rule.statement: Treat zero visible competitors with suspicion, not as opportunity. Low competition may mean low demand. Require a problem-validation step before proceeding to build when the competitor count is 0-2.
rule.rationale: The correlation between zero competitors and zero demand is non-trivial. Blue ocean and empty desert look identical until you start selling.

section.name: audience-accessibility
section.p: Score five accessibility factors 1-5 (target average greater than 3.5):

list.item: Organic reach: can you reach them without paid ads (Reddit, Twitter, LinkedIn)?
Community concentration: do they hang out in specific identifiable places?
Media presence: are there podcasts, newsletters, or blogs in this niche?
Paid acquisition: can you buy ads to reach them at reasonable CPM?
Purchasing power: do they have budget authority and willingness to spend $50+/month?

antipattern.description: Agent scores accessibility 4-5 for any niche with a Reddit subreddit, regardless of engagement.
antipattern.reason: Subreddit existence is not a signal of buyer concentration. Many subreddits are low-activity communities of casual interest, not decision-makers with purchasing authority.

rule.statement: Use 30-day post velocity and subscriber-to-post ratio to weight Reddit accessibility scores, not raw subscriber count.
rule.rationale: A 500k-member subreddit with 2 posts per day is less accessible than a 20k-member niche community with 50 posts per day. Velocity indicates active discussion and reachable buyers.

rule.statement: Score buyer (decision-maker) and user (practitioner) separately when they differ. Agents collapse them; a manager-buyer/developer-user niche requires two distinct accessibility and monetization analyses.
rule.rationale: Pricing and distribution strategy differ entirely depending on who signs the check vs. who uses the product. A tool loved by developers but purchased by non-technical managers requires enterprise positioning and a different GTM than a developer self-service product.

section.name: scoring-formula
section.p: Niche Score = (Market x 0.25) + (Competition x 0.20) + (Accessibility x 0.15) + (Monetization x 0.20) + (Personal Fit x 0.20)
Decision bands: greater than 4.0 = Excellent, proceed immediately; 3.5-4.0 = Good, validate further; 3.0-3.5 = Risky, improve weak areas; less than 3.0 = Reconsider or pivot.

rule.statement: Personal Fit is never scored by an agent. It is founder self-report only. Any niche where founder excitement is below 3 fails regardless of weighted total score.
rule.rationale: No agent has access to the founder's actual daily energy, emotional resilience, or domain curiosity. These are the determinants of execution quality over the 12-24 months before product-market fit. They cannot be estimated from a skills list.

rule.statement: Calibrate decision-band thresholds against your own past wins and losses, not the framework defaults. If your last winning niche scored 3.7, your proceed threshold should be 3.5+, not 4.0.
rule.rationale: The 4.0 "proceed immediately" threshold is a starting default, not a universal constant. Each founder's scoring patterns have systematic biases that emerge after a few evaluation cycles.

rule.statement: Never let the same agent gather evidence and score in the same context. Use niche-researcher (evidence phase) and niche-scorer (scoring phase) as separate agents with separate contexts.
rule.rationale: An agent that gathered evidence for a niche it "researched" will score more favorably than one given the same evidence cold. Separation eliminates the collection bias.

list.item: Personal Fit row: ALWAYS founder. No agent delegation.
Final go/no-go decision: ALWAYS human. Agent produces scored comparison; founder decides.
TAM/SAM numbers: reject any without source_url. Better unknown than fabricated.
Currency: all TAM figures must have explicit currency notation. Agents omit FX/PPP by default.
One sentence niche definition mandatory: [Product] for [Audience] who [Problem]. If you cannot, agent output is worthless.
Evidence-before-score discipline: never let the same agent gather + score; bias compounds.
Long-tail competitor scan: agents miss IH/Etsy/Discord competitors. Force a "what would a $1k MRR solo developer build here" prompt as separate pass.
Calibrate thresholds against your wins: if your last winning niche scored 3.7, your "proceed" band is 3.5+, not 4.0.
Re-evaluate quarterly: market direction (Trends arrow) matters more than absolute size for early-stage.
Personal fit overrides: any niche where founder excitement less than 3 fails, regardless of score. Hard-code this gate.
Buyer not equal to user: niches where buyer (e.g., manager) differs from user (e.g., engineer) need both columns scored separately. Agents collapse them; force split.
TAM hallucination is the #1 failure. Reject any number without source_url. Better "unknown" than fabricated.
Agents over-rate "blue ocean" — they don't know what they don't see. Probability of zero competitors ≈ probability of zero demand. Score caution.
Reddit member count is vanity; weight by 30-day post velocity instead. Force agent to fetch both.
Monetization scoring biases toward SaaS (training data dominance). Course/template/services niches systematically under-score; correct manually.
Human-in-loop checkpoint: personal-fit row + final go/no-go always human.
Currency: TAM in USD, but if niche is non-US, agents miss FX/PPP. Mark currency explicitly.
"Few competitors" can mean either blue ocean or no demand — forces a problem-validation step before commit.
Agents conflate niche size with founder addressable market; the 1-3% SOM is on a multi-year horizon, not Year 1.

template.purpose: Full Niche Evaluation Scorecard with all 5 criteria, source columns, and decision block.
Phase-1 evidence-gathering agent prompt (no scoring; citations required).
Phase-2 scoring agent prompt (evidence-only, refuse-without-source rule).
